description: Use for autonomous end-to-end implementation of a SINGLE spec, task card, GitHub issue, or feature description: ingest the requirement, then plan with TDD, run review loops and quality gates, and open one PR. Triggers on "run the agentic loop", "implement this issue", "ralph this", "take this from spec to PR", "build this out end to end", "implement this spec and open a PR", a GitHub Action invoking the agent with an issue payload — and on ANY hand-off of a spec/issue/feature for full implementation, even when the loop is not named. Strongly prefer this skill over plain plan-mode planning or the brainstorming skill whenever a concrete spec or issue is handed off to build end-to-end: it plans AND implements AND ships, not just explores or plans. Do NOT use for trivial one-line/typo/config edits, pure research or Q&A with no code deliverable, multi-issue or cross-repo work, or merging/deploying.

results.query: Take issue #123 and drive it through to a PR. The spec is in the issue body.
Here's a spec (spec.md attached). Take it from spec to PR — full pipeline, TDD, review loops, the lot.
Run ralph on this task card: we need a CLI command `myapp scaffold <name>` that copies the template directory and renames it.
Here's the feature description for a token-bucket rate limiter (60 req/min per API key, sliding window, returns 429 with Retry-After). Build this out end to end and open a PR when it's done.
Take the requirements in issue #204 and just get it all the way to a pull request — plan it, test it, review it, ship the PR.
I've written up a spec in SPEC.md for the new export pipeline. Implement this spec and open a PR.
Can you handle GH-88 end to end for me? Everything's specced in the issue.
there's a design doc at docs/rfcs/0007-idempotency-keys.md, it's pretty complete. i want you to actually build it — write the tests first, implement, review it properly, and open a PR against main
got a github issue (#312) describing a csv export endpoint with date-range filters. spec's solid, no open questions. run the whole thing for me start to finish — plan, tdd, self-review, pr

ok the ticket LINEAR-441 has the full writeup for the new webhook retry logic — exponential backoff, max 5 attempts, dead-letter after that. can you just take it from here and get a PR up? tests and all
Fix the typo in README.md line 12 — it says 'recieve' instead of 'receive'.
How does the agentic-loop skill decide which planner agent to dispatch when there's no roster configured?
Let's build a new notifications feature — I haven't figured out what it should even do yet. Help me think through the options.
Read through the auth spec in docs/auth.md and tell me whether the token-refresh design is sound — I just want your assessment, don't build anything.
The spec-to-PR loop already opened PR #305 — can you merge it and deploy to staging?
Implement dark mode across all three of our repos (web, mobile, docs) and open PRs on each.
can you review PR #77 for me? it implements the new billing webhook handler and i want a thorough code review of the diff before i approve and merge it
bump the version in package.json to 2.4.0 and add a matching entry at the top of CHANGELOG.md
i've got a spec for the notification service in NOTIFY_SPEC.md — can you validate it and tell me if it's watertight enough to start building? not asking you to build it yet
our CI is failing on the main branch after the last merge — the audit-log test times out intermittently. can you dig in and figure out what's causing the flake?
